0.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
text label for the term that is useful to human user
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation
Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, variants).
Broader concepts in other schemes.
Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Note documenting a change.
Concepts in other schemes with similar meaning.
Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.
Note for editors and maintainers.
Concepts in other schemes with equivalent meaning.
Example of use of this concept.
Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.
Note about past state or use.
Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.
Narrower concepts in other schemes.
Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Classification code for this concept within a scheme.
General note about this concept.
Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
Associatively related concepts.
Related concepts in other schemes.
Note clarifying meaning and/or intended use.
Concept scheme(s) for which this is a top concept.

1.description: The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).
The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.
True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.
The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.

properties.description: Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.
Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.
CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.
Per-category Statistic value objects.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.
Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).
Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).
Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).
Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.
reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset
The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.
The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).

The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Per-category Statistic value objects.
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.
The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).
Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.
Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).
Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).
Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).

CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.
The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.
True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.
The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.
The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.
CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.
Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).
The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).
Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).

Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.

Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.
identifier or name for the data type concept.
Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).
URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).
maximum numeric value in the dataset
minimum numeric value in the dataset
URI or code identifying the unit of measure
unit of measurement as text
identifier or name for the property concept
Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.
string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization
